Now, implement DSU with path compression and union by size.

Now, code in C++:

So, in C++, inside the minSwapsCouples function:

So, the code outline:

class Solution {

public:

    int minSwapsCouples(vector<int>& row) {

        int n = row.size() / 2;

        vector<int> parent(n);

        vector<int> size(n, 1);

        // Initialize parent array

        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {

            parent[i] = i;

        }

        // Find function

        function<int(int)> find = [&](int x) {

            while (x != parent[x]) {

                parent[x] = parent[parent[x]];

                x = parent[x];

            }

            return x;

        };

        // Union function

        for (int i = 0; i < row.size(); i += 2) {

            int a = row[i];

            int b = row[i+1];

            int x = a / 2;

            int y = b / 2;

            if (x != y) {

                int rootX = find(x);

                int rootY = find(y);

                if (rootX != rootY) {

                    parent[rootY] = rootX;

                    size[rootX] += size[rootY];

                }

            }

        }

        // Now calculate the total swaps

        unordered_set<int> roots;

        int swaps = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {

            int root = find(i);

            if (roots.find(root) == roots.end()) {

                roots.insert(root);

                swaps += (size[root] - 1);

            }

        }

        return swaps;

    }

};

i=2: a=0 → x=0. b=2 → 2//2=1. find(x=0's root is 0. find(y=1's root is 0. same. no action. 

Then, for the roots, process i=0 and 1. find(0) is 0. find(1) is 0. So only add root 0. swaps += 2-1=1. Correct.

So this code seems to work.

Time complexity: O(N), where N is the number of elements in row. Because for the DSU operations, each find and union is O(alpha(N)) time, which is nearly constant. The final loop is O(n), where n is the number of couples (row.size()/2). So overall O(N).

Space complexity: O(n) for the DSU data structures.

class Solution {

public:

    int minSwapsCouples(vector<int>& row) {

        int n = row.size() / 2;

        vector<int> parent(n);

        vector<int> size(n, 1);

        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {

            parent[i] = i;

        }

        function<int(int)> find = [&](int x) {

            while (x != parent[x]) {

                parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]; // path compression

                x = parent[x];

            }

            return x;

        };

        for (int i = 0; i < row.size(); i += 2) {

            int a = row[i];

            int b = row[i + 1];

            int x = a / 2;

            int y = b / 2;

            if (x != y) {

                int rootX = find(x);

                int rootY = find(y);

                if (rootX != rootY) {

                    parent[rootY] = rootX; // merge

                    size[rootX] += size[rootY];

                }

            }

        }

        unordered_set<int> roots;

        int swaps = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {

            int root = find(i);

            if (roots.find(root) == roots.end()) {

                roots.insert(root);

                swaps += (size[root] - 1);

            }

        }

        return swaps;

    }

};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int minSwapsCouples(vector<int>& row) {
        int n = row.size() / 2;
        vector<int> parent(n);
        vector<int> size(n, 1);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            parent[i] = i;
        }
        function<int(int)> find = [&](int x) {
            while (x != parent[x]) {
                parent[x] = parent[parent[x]];
                x = parent[x];
            }
            return x;
        };
        for (int i = 0; i < row.size(); i += 2) {
            int a = row[i];
            int b = row[i + 1];
            int x = a / 2;
            int y = b / 2;
            if (x != y) {
                int rootX = find(x);
                int rootY = find(y);
                if (rootX != rootY) {
                    parent[rootY] = rootX;
                    size[rootX] += size[rootY];
                }
            }
        }
        unordered_set<int> roots;
        int swaps = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            int root = find(i);
            if (roots.find(root) == roots.end()) {
                roots.insert(root);
                swaps += (size[root] - 1);
            }
        }
        return swaps;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution models the problem as a graph where each node represents a couple. Using Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union), we connect couples that are incorrectly paired. Each connected component contributes (size - 1) swaps. This approach ensures minimal swaps by resolving cycles efficiently. Strengths include optimal time and space complexity; weaknesses are limited to integer division for couple identification.
